publications.title: Molecular Basis for the Recognition of HIV Nef138-8 Epitope by a Pair of Human Public T Cell Receptors.
publications.authors: Ma K, Chai Y, Guan J, Tan S, Qi J, Kawana-Tachikawa A, Dong T, Iwamoto A, Shi Y, Gao GF.
publications.executive_summary: Two public human TCRs (TD08 and H25-11), isolated from White and Japanese patients respectively, recognize the immunodominant HLA-A24-restricted Nef138-8 epitope from HIV Nef protein with similar gene usage patterns (TRAV8-3/TRAJ10 and TRBV7-9/TRBD1*01/TRBJ2-5). Crystal structures of both TCR–HLA-A24–peptide complexes reveal that recognition depends predominantly on TCR contacts with the HLA scaffold rather than the peptide itself, allowing both TCRs to tolerate a Y139F immune-escape mutation prevalent in Japanese HIV populations. This cross-reactivity against an escaping variant is clinically significant because public TCRs targeting conserved epitopes may help control disease progression and limit the success of viral escape. The findings establish a structural basis for public TCR recognition of HIV epitopes and support the rationale for engineering these TCRs into T cells for therapeutic immunotherapy against persistent or evolving HIV infection.
